questions.question:
The table shows favorite subjects by grade. How many 11th graders prefer Science?

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Math} & \text{English} & \text{Science} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{9th} & 28 & 16 & 20 & 64 \\ \hline \text{10th} & 22 & 34 & 16 & 72 \\ \hline \text{11th} & 15 & 25 & 24 & 64 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 65 & 75 & 60 & 200 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Find the intersection of the '11th' row and 'Science' column. The joint frequency is 24.

questions.question:
The table shows favorite subjects by grade. What is the marginal frequency for students who prefer English?

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Math} & \text{English} & \text{Science} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{9th} & 28 & 16 & 20 & 64 \\ \hline \text{10th} & 22 & 34 & 16 & 72 \\ \hline \text{11th} & 15 & 25 & 24 & 64 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 65 & 75 & 60 & 200 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: The marginal frequency for English is the column total: 16 + 34 + 25 = 75.

questions.question:
The table shows favorite subjects by grade. What percentage of 10th graders prefer Math? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Math} & \text{English} & \text{Science} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{9th} & 28 & 16 & 20 & 64 \\ \hline \text{10th} & 22 & 34 & 16 & 72 \\ \hline \text{11th} & 15 & 25 & 24 & 64 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 65 & 75 & 60 & 200 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: 10th graders who prefer Math = 22. Total 10th graders = 72. Percentage = (22 ÷ 72) × 100 ≈ 30.6%, which rounds to 31%.

questions.question:
The table shows club participation by gender. How many girls are in Band or Choir?

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Drama} & \text{Choir} & \text{Band} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Boys} & 15 & 30 & 20 & 65 \\ \hline \text{Girls} & 35 & 20 & 30 & 85 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 50 & 50 & 50 & 150 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Girls in Band = 30. Girls in Choir = 20. Since no student is in both, total = 30 + 20 = 50.

questions.question:
The table shows club participation by gender. What fraction of the students in Drama are boys? Express in simplest form.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Drama} & \text{Choir} & \text{Band} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Boys} & 15 & 30 & 20 & 65 \\ \hline \text{Girls} & 35 & 20 & 30 & 85 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 50 & 50 & 50 & 150 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Boys in Drama = 15. Total in Drama = 50. Fraction = 15/50 = 3/10.

questions.question:
The table shows how students get to school. What percentage of students do NOT take the bus? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Car} & \text{Bus} & \text{Walk} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{7th} & 24 & 40 & 16 & 80 \\ \hline \text{8th} & 36 & 30 & 24 & 90 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 60 & 70 & 40 & 170 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Total bus riders = 70. Students not taking bus = 170 - 70 = 100. Percentage = (100 ÷ 170) × 100 ≈ 58.8%, rounds to 59%.

questions.question:
The table shows how students get to school. Among 7th graders, what fraction walk to school? Express in simplest form.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Car} & \text{Bus} & \text{Walk} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{7th} & 24 & 40 & 16 & 80 \\ \hline \text{8th} & 36 & 30 & 24 & 90 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 60 & 70 & 40 & 170 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

The table shows elective choices. How many students take Art or Music (or both)?

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Art} & \text{Music} & \text{Neither} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Seniors} & 35 & 45 & 20 & 100 \\ \hline \text{Juniors} & 40 & 30 & 30 & 100 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 75 & 75 & 50 & 200 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Total students = 200. Students taking Neither = 50. Students taking Art or Music = 200 - 50 = 150.

questions.question:
The table shows elective choices. What percentage of Juniors take Music? Round to the nearest percent.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Art} & \text{Music} & \text{Neither} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Seniors} & 35 & 45 & 20 & 100 \\ \hline \text{Juniors} & 40 & 30 & 30 & 100 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 75 & 75 & 50 & 200 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Juniors in Music = 30. Total Juniors = 100. Percentage = 30/100 = 30%.

questions.question:
The table shows movie preferences by gender. How many more men than women prefer Action?

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|c|}\hline & \text{Comedy} & \text{Action} & \text{Drama} & \text{Total} \\ \hline \text{Men} & 28 & 42 & 10 & 80 \\ \hline \text{Women} & 32 & 18 & 30 & 80 \\ \hline \text{Total} & 60 & 60 & 40 & 160 \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: Men who prefer Action = 42. Women who prefer Action = 18. Difference = 42 - 18 = 24.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can read, interpret, and calculate with two-way frequency tables at a high level.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Keep practicing conditional percentages and 'or' problems — read carefully whether the denominator is a row total, column total, or grand total.
scoreMessages.50: Review the difference between joint frequencies (inner cells), marginal frequencies (totals), and conditional percentages (part divided by row or column total).
scoreMessages.0: Start by locating the correct cell for joint frequencies. For percentages, identify the correct total to use as the denominator. For 'or' problems, add the categories and subtract any overlap.
